title: The Nixie of the Mill-Pond
lines: A miller had a beautiful daughter and a mill by a pond.
A water spirit offered him riches if he would give her whatever was born in the mill that year.
The miller, thinking it would be a young animal, agreed.
But it turned out to be a daughter.
Years passed, and on her eighteenth birthday, the nixie came to claim her.
The girl fled into the forest.
A woodcutter found her and took her in.
A young prince fell in love with her and married her.
One day she went to bathe in a stream, and the nixie pulled her under.
The prince, grief-stricken, sought advice from a wise woman.
She told him how to capture the nixie and force her to release his wife.
He did as instructed, and the nixie was forced to return the girl.
Together they lived happily ever after, safe from the water spirit's curse.
